title: How LIGO Hears Colliding Black Holes
script_summary: Explains how LIGO detects gravitational waves from colliding black holes using laser interferometry.

segments.voiceover: In 2015, Earth felt spacetime ripple — and LIGO heard it.
These ripples are called gravitational waves, caused by massive cosmic collisions.
LIGO uses laser interferometry to detect them — a method that measures tiny changes in distance.
The first detection was GW150914, a collision of black holes over a billion light-years away.
This incredible technology lets us hear the universe's violent symphonies.
Want to dive deeper into the cosmos? Like and follow for more!

full_voiceover: In 2015, Earth felt spacetime ripple — and LIGO heard it. These ripples are called gravitational waves, caused by massive cosmic collisions. LIGO uses laser interferometry to detect them — a method that measures tiny changes in distance. The first detection was GW150914, a collision of black holes over a billion light-years away. This incredible technology lets us hear the universe's violent symphonies. Want to dive deeper into the cosmos? Like and follow for more!
